executive_summary: Analysis of 1,991 grid cells across the Bukidnon-Mindanao corridor reveals that 2.0% (40 cells, approximately 968 hectares) are classified as high deforestation risk based on 2020-2022 loss predictions from 2001-2019 feature data. The primary risk drivers are historical loss rate acceleration (29.1% importance) and proximity to existing deforestation fronts (23.9% importance), indicating that deforestation pressure is concentrated along expanding frontiers rather than distributed uniformly.
notable_findings: 2.0% of cells are high-risk — concentrated along the Bukidnon-Davao del Norte boundary
Loss acceleration is the second strongest predictor — deforestation is intensifying in this corridor
recommendations: Prioritize DENR Region XI enforcement along the Bukidnon-Davao boundary where loss acceleration exceeds the regional average, targeting the 3 identified hotspot coordinates.
Review compliance of agricultural expansion permits under DENR Administrative Order 2003-30 in areas where frontier distance is below 5km.
Coordinate with the National Commission on Indigenous Peoples (NCIP) to verify that ancestral domain boundaries in the Bukidnon highlands are not being encroached by commercial operations.
Deploy community-based forest monitoring in partnership with the local CENRO, prioritizing cells with >70% risk probability for quarterly ground-truth surveys.

data_provenance.source: Derived from Hansen GFW loss_year (2001-2019 only)
